message:
perf(indexer): optional CASS_PREP_PROFILE step timings across rebuild_tantivy_from_db prepare leg

Instrumentation-only patch. Earlier benchmarking (facd9df2,
CASS_INDEXING_HISTORICAL_BENCHMARK_RESULTS.md) established that the
prepare leg of `rebuild_tantivy_from_db` is the dominant remaining
fixed cost (~24s per rebuild vs. ~49s index window). We want to
attribute that 24s to specific substeps without leaving the
logging on in production — which is why the instrumentation is
gated behind an env var.

When `CASS_PREP_PROFILE` is set, emit a `CASS_PREP_PROFILE step=...
step_ms=N total_ms=M` line to stderr after each substep in the
prepare leg (read-only open, source list, agent/workspace
lookups, db-state hashing, existing-checkpoint probe, etc.). When
unset, every callsite is a single `is_some()` check plus a no-op
closure invocation — no measurable overhead, zero output.

Usage:
  CASS_PREP_PROFILE=1 cass --db ... index --force-rebuild
  # then grep stderr for `CASS_PREP_PROFILE step=...`

Values from a single invocation are enough to rank substeps by
wall time. Aggregate across several runs for stable medians.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
